auth_type: Basic Auth or OAuth2 via IWS
auth_notes: Requires the Integrated Web Services (IWS) server to be configured on the IBM i partition. Authentication is typically handled via Basic Auth against the OS/400 user profile or via a modern API gateway/middleware.
rate_limits: Determined by system resources, job queue settings, and IWS container configuration; no standard public API limits.
gotchas: Character encoding issues between EBCDIC (AS/400) and UTF-8 (n8n/Web)
Strict firewall/VPN requirements as these systems are rarely exposed to the public internet
Legacy RPG/COBOL programs often require custom wrappers to be compatible with RESTful structures
client_must_provide: VPN or SSH Tunnel access to the internal network
research_notes: Integration requires using the HTTP Request node to interface with the IBM i Integrated Web Services (IWS) REST API. Users must first deploy their RPG/COBOL programs or SQL statements as REST services using the IBM Web Administration for i interface. Alternatively, a middleware layer like MuleSoft or a custom Node.js bridge using 'idb-connector' may be required.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration requires using the HTTP Request node to interface with the IBM i Integrated Web Services (IWS) REST API. Users must first deploy their RPG/COBOL programs or SQL statements as REST services using the IBM Web Administration for i interface. Alternatively, a middleware layer like MuleSoft or a custom Node.js bridge using 'idb-connector' may be required.
